context.game: Pico Echo - virtual pet cockatiel simulation
context.role: Autonomous grooming behavior, randomly triggered during idle
context.screen_position: Center at x=64, y=60, on perch at y=80
context.usage_scenario: 30% chance to trigger from idle state, plays for 5 seconds showing self-grooming behavior

visual_specification.shape: Cockatiel with head turned to side, beak touching wing/body feathers
visual_specification.texture: Pixel art with organic hand-drawn feel, no gradients
visual_specification.outline: 1px dark outline (#1D2B53) on crest, beak, and body silhouette

animation.description: Frame 1: head turns left, Frame 2: beak touches left wing, Frame 3: head center, Frame 4: head turns right, Frame 5: beak touches right wing

visual_specification.details: Head rotates approximately 30° left and right, beak makes contact with wing area (visible as beak tip touching body), crest in normal position, one eye visible when head turns (2x2px), wing feathers slightly ruffled during contact

relationships.shared_with: Same color palette as all Echo states
relationships.differences_from: echo_idle (head rotation and beak position), echo_sleeping (eyes open and active movement)

explicit_negations: No dirt or dust removal visual
No wing spreading beyond normal body width
No interaction with player during preening (autonomous only)
